description: The Heat Shock Response (HSR) in *Saccharomyces cerevisiae* is a fundamental cellular defense mechanism against proteotoxic stress, primarily triggered by elevated temperatures but also by other stressors like oxidative or osmotic stress. The process begins with the accumulation of misfolded proteins, which titrate away Hsp90/Hsp70 chaperones, leading to the release and activation of the Heat Shock Factor 1 (Hsf1). Hsf1 trimerizes, undergoes phosphorylation, and binds to Heat Shock Elements (HSEs) in the promoters of heat shock protein (HSP) genes, initiating their transcription and subsequent translation into new chaperones.
mermaid:
graph TD
    A[Heat Shock / Other Stressors] --> B{Is Stress acute/severe?}
    B -->|Yes| C[Protein Misfolding & Accumulation]
    B -->|No| D[Cellular Homeostasis Maintained]

    C --> E[Hsp90/Hsp70 Chaperones Bind Misfolded Proteins]
    E --> F[Release of Hsf1 from Chaperones]
    F --> G[Hsf1 Monomer]
    G --> H[Hsf1 Trimerization]
    H --> I[Hsf1 Phosphorylation]
    J((Hsf1 Fully Activated))
    H & I --> J
    J --> K[Binding to Heat Shock Elements (HSEs)]
    K --> L[Transcriptional Activation of HSP Genes]
    L --> M[HSP mRNA Synthesis]
    M --> N[HSP mRNA Translation]
    N --> O[Synthesis of Heat Shock Proteins (HSPs)]
    O --> P[Hsp Chaperones (e.g., Ssa1/2, Hsp90)]

    C --> Q[Partially Folded/Misfolded Proteins]
    Q --> R{Is Protein Refoldable by Cytosolic Chaperones?}
    R -->|Yes| S[ATP-dependent Refolding by Chaperones (P)]
    S --> T[Properly Folded Proteins]
    R -->|No| U{Is Protein Terminally Misfolded or Heavily Aggregated?}
    U -->|Yes| V{Degradation via Proteasome OR Mitochondria?}
    U -->|No| W[Protein Aggregation / Inclusion Body Formation]

    V --> X[Ubiquitination of Terminally Misfolded Proteins]
    X --> Y[/26S Proteasome Degradation/]
    Y --> Z[Amino Acids / Peptides]

    V --> AA[Delivery to Mitochondria by Cytosolic Hsp70 (Ssa1/2)]
    AA --> AB[Binding to TOM Complex (Outer Membrane)]
    AB --> AC[Translocation through TOM/TIM Complexes]
    AC --> AD[Mitochondrial Hsp70 (Ssc1) Binding]
    AD --> AE[ATP-dependent Import into Mitochondrial Matrix]
    AE --> AF{Refoldable in Mitochondria?}
    AF -->|Yes| AG[Refolding by mtHsp60 / mtHsp70]
    AG --> AH[Properly Folded Mitochondrial Proteins]
    AF -->|No| AI[/Degradation by Mitochondrial AAA-ATPases (Yta10/12)/]
    AI --> AJ[Mitochondrial Peptides]

    A --> BK[Osmotic Stress Component (from A)]
    BK --> BL[Glycerol 3-phosphate dehydrogenase (Gpd1) Activity]
    BL --> BM[Glycerol Synthesis]
    BL --> BN[Modulation of Hsf1 Activation]
    BN --> J

    BO[Gpd1 Deletion / Impaired Activity] --> BP[Reduced Hsf1 Activation]
    BP --> BQ[Reduced Thermotolerance]

    B --> BR[Acute/Severe Stress for Phase Separation (from B)]
    BR --> BS[Specific mRNAs / RNA-binding Proteins]
    BS --> BT[Stress-Triggered Liquid-Liquid Phase Separation]
    BT --> BU[Formation of Stress Granules / P-bodies]
    BU --> BV[Sequestration of Non-essential mRNA/Proteins]
    BU --> BW[Reduced Protein Synthesis]

    P --> BX[Chaperones Bind to Hsf1]
    BX --> BY[Hsf1 Inactivation / Dephosphorylation]
    BY --> BZ[Reduced HSP Gene Expression]

    T --> CA[Cellular Adaptation / Survival]
    AH --> CA
    Z --> CA
    AJ --> CA
    BW --> CA
    D --> CA

    W --> CB{Protein Quality Control Overwhelmed?}
    CB -->|Yes| CC[Cell Death / Apoptosis]
    CB -->|No| CA

    BQ --> CC

    CA --> CD[Long-term Adaptation / Homeostasis]
    CC --> CE[Cellular Failure]
